content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN vertices.

Initialize a variable X=0X = 0X=0. You would like to perform the following operation N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 times:


Choose an edge (u,v)(u,v)(u,v) that still hasn't been deleted.
Delete the edge (u,v)(u,v)(u,v).
Let siz(u)\text{siz}(u)siz(u) denote the size of the connected component that uuu is contained in and siz(v)\text{siz}(v)siz(v) denote the size of the connected component that vvv is contained in. XOR the variable XXX with either siz(u)\text{siz}(u)siz(u) or siz(v)\text{siz}(v)siz(v). More formally, do any **one** of the following 222 operations:

X=X‚äïsiz(u)X = X \oplus \text{siz}(u)X=X‚äïsiz(u)
X=X‚äïsiz(v)X = X \oplus \text{siz}(v)X=X‚äïsiz(v)


Find the **minimum** possible value of XXX that can be achieved after performing all N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 operations.

Additionally, provide a valid sequence of operations that achieves this minimum value of XXX. If there are multiple sequences of operations that achieve the minimum value of XXX, you may output any of them.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN ‚Äî the number of vertices of the tree.
The next N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 lines describe the edges. The iii-th of these N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui‚Äã and viv_ivi‚Äã, denoting an edge between uiu_iui‚Äã and viv_ivi‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output NNN lines.



On the first line, print the minimum possible value of XXX.
On the iii-th of the next N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 lines, output three space-separated integers uiu_iui‚Äã, viv_ivi‚Äã, xix_ixi‚Äã, where one of xi=uix_i = u_ixi‚Äã=ui‚Äã or xi=vix_i = v_ixi‚Äã=vi‚Äã holds - denoting that the edge (ui,vi)(u_i,v_i)(ui‚Äã,vi‚Äã) is deleted on the iii-th operation, after which XXX is set to X‚äïsiz(xi)X \oplus \text{siz}(x_i)X‚äïsiz(xi‚Äã).

If there are multiple sequences of operations that minimize XXX, you may output any of them.
constraints: The input graph describes a tree.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

Delete the edge (1,2)(1,2)(1,2) and select node 111.

X=X‚äïsiz(1)=0‚äï1=1X = X \oplus \text{siz}(1) = 0 \oplus 1 = 1X=X‚äïsiz(1)=0‚äï1=1.



Test case 222:

Delete the edge (3,1)(3,1)(3,1) and select node 333.

X=X‚äïsiz(3)=0‚äï1=1X = X \oplus \text{siz}(3) = 0 \oplus 1 = 1X=X‚äïsiz(3)=0‚äï1=1.


Cut the edge (1,2)(1,2)(1,2) and select node 222.

X=X‚äïsiz(2)=1‚äï1=0X = X \oplus \text{siz}(2) = 1 \oplus 1 = 0X=X‚äïsiz(2)=1‚äï1=0.
